		<h1 class="entry-title">28 Funny Posts That Brilliantly Satirize LinkedIn By Ken Cheng</h1><div class="entry-meta entry-meta-divider-vline">
	<span class="posted-by"><span class="author-avatar"><a class="author-image" href="https://pleated-jeans.com/author/alex-buscemi/" aria-label="Author image"><picture><source srcset="https://pleated-jeans.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/1683481039639-96x96.webp " type="image/webp"><img src="https://pleated-jeans.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/1683481039639-96x96.jpeg" height="25" width="25" class="avatar avatar-25 wp-user-avatar wp-user-avatar-25 alignnone photo sp-no-webp" alt="Alex Buscemi" loading="eager" fetchpriority="high" decoding="async"> </picture></a><span class="image-size-ratio"></span></span><span class="meta-label">By</span><span class="author vcard"><a class="url fn n" href="https://pleated-jeans.com/author/alex-buscemi/">Alex Buscemi</a></span></span>					<span class="posted-on">
						<time class="entry-date published updated" datetime="2025-05-03T07:38:26-05:00" itemprop="dateModified">May 3, 2025</time>					</span>
					</div><!-- .entry-meta -->
</header><!-- .entry-header -->

<div class="entry-content single-content" data-content-ads-inserted="true" style="overflow: visible !important;">
	<p>Why did I stop sleeping?</p>
<p>Because success doesn’t rest.</p>
<p>Why do I bring my resumé to funerals?</p>
<p>Because every moment is a networking opportunity.</p>
<p>Why am I obsessed with these posts by <a href="https://pleated-jeans.com/2025/03/01/witty-idiot-stephen-szczerba-tweets/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">comedian</a> Ken Cheng?</p>
<p>Because they get it.</p>
<p>Or rather — they make fun of people who <em>think</em> they get it.</p>
<p>Ken Cheng looked at <a href="https://pleated-jeans.com/2025/04/24/funny-insane-linkedin-posts/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">LinkedIn</a>.</p>
<p>And said: enough is enough.</p>
<p data-slot-rendered-content="true">No more 900-word posts about getting <a href="https://pleated-jeans.com/2024/09/19/reddit-employees-fired-first-day/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">fired</a> and calling it a “gift.”</p>
<div class="mv-ad-box" data-slotid="content_btf" style="height: 350px; width: max(300px, 100%); overflow: clip !important;"><div class="mv-rail-frame-350" data-slotid="content_btf" style="overflow: visible !important;"><div class="mv-rail-slide-350" data-slotid="content_btf" style="overflow: visible !important;"><div class="mv-rail-sticky-350" data-slotid="content_btf"><div id="content_btf_wrapper" class="adunitwrapper content_btf_wrapper" data-wrapper="content_btf" data-nosnippet="">
          <div id="content_btf" class="content_btf adunit">
            
          </div>
        </div></div></div></div>
  
        
    
</div>

<p>No more “Let that sink in.”</p>
<p>These posts are brutal.</p>
<p>They’re hilarious.</p>
<p>They’re what happens when someone finally tells LinkedIn to go outside and touch grass.</p>
<p>Scroll through.</p>
<p>Reflect.</p>
<p>And then post a comment about how this reminded you of a time you mentored a struggling CEO at age 12.</p>
<p>Let’s dive in.</p>
<p>Follow Ken Cheng: <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/ken-cheng-991849b6/" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="external">LinkedIn</a> | <a href="https://www.instagram.com/kenchengcomedy/" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="external">Instagram</a> | <a href="https://x.com/kenchengcomedy" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="external">Twitter</a> | <a href="https://www.facebook.com/kenchengcomedy/" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="external">Facebook</a></p>
